title: driver init/transfer recovery path (mmio)
statement: From docs/basket/spec-mmio.md §3.1/§3.4/§3.5 (the driver recovery algorithm). The device is modeled by a scripted STATUS stream: `struct Dev { st: [24]u32, len: u32, pos: u32 }`, and each STATUS read returns `st[pos]` then advances `pos`. STATUS bits: READY=0x01, BUSY=0x02, DONE=0x04, ERROR=0x08, TIMEOUT=0x10. Define: `fn read_status(d: write Dev) -> u32` (return `st[pos]`, then `pos += 1`); `fn poll_ready(d: write Dev) -> u32` — read STATUS up to MAX_POLLS=8 times, returning 0 (OK) as soon as READY is set, 1 (TIMEOUT) if TIMEOUT is set or the 8 polls are exhausted without READY; `fn do_transfer(d: write Dev) -> u32` — loop reading STATUS (bounded by a stall count of 8): return 1 (XFER_ERROR) if ERROR set, 2 (XFER_TIMEOUT) if TIMEOUT set or stall exhausted, 0 (OK) if DONE set; `fn run(d: write Dev) -> i64` implementing §3.5: poll_ready for init; if it TIMEOUTs, poll_ready once more to recover and return -1 (DEV_FATAL) if that also TIMEOUTs; then do_transfer; if that is non-OK, recover (poll_ready; -1 if it TIMEOUTs) and retry do_transfer ONCE; return 0 if the (retried) transfer is OK, else -1. Exactly one retry per recovery path. Define only these items.
